description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a complex, multi-gabled roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The front facade features a mix of stone and brick textures, with large rectangular windows and a prominent entryway that includes a small overhang and steps leading up to the door. The right side of the house has a garage door, while the left side is mostly plain with minimal window openings. The rear of the house includes a covered porch supported by columns, accessible by a set of stairs, and several windows of varying sizes. The structure is elevated on piers at the back, suggesting adaptation for uneven terrain or flood-prone areas. The overall design blends traditional and modern suburban architectural elements, with a focus on symmetry and functional outdoor spaces.
description: A two-story suburban house with a multi-gabled shingle roof, stone and brick facade, large windows, a front entryway with steps, and a rear covered porch on piers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with steps, large windows, and a garage door on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the plain wall with minimal windows and the extension of the roof over the rear porch.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the covered porch on piers, multiple windows, and a secondary entry with steps.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the garage extension, side windows, and the continuation of the porch structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large rectangular windows and a bay window
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway with a small overhang, steps, and a single front door framed by stone and brick accents
